question: Starmer out before July?
textDescription:
Resolves according to this Polymarket:

This market will resolve to “Yes” if Keir Starmer ceases to be the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for any period of time between September 14, 2025, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Keir Starmer's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of the UK, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.
newsShortReason: Starmer's recent defiance against resignation calls and lack of leadership challenge suggest he will remain Prime Minister until June 30, 2026.
newsKeyEvidence: Starmer rejected calls to resign after local election losses, stating he will 'get on with governing'. | No leadership challenge has been initiated within the Labour Party. | Starmer's Chief of Staff and Director of Communications resigned in February 2026 over the Mandelson scandal. | Despite internal pressures, Starmer has maintained his position and focus on governing. | Andy Burnham's candidacy in the Makerfield by-election indicates potential future leadership challenges, but not immediate resignation.
